name: Prismic

unions.name: FieldType
unions.comment: The type of values for a Prismic form field.

unions.name: Model
unions.comment: The Prismic Model keeps track of configuration and holds the response cache.

unions.name: Predicate
unions.comment: The type representing Prismic query predicates.

unions.name: Ref
unions.comment: A type to disambiguate `Ref`s from other `String`s.

aliases.name: Api
aliases.comment:
The `Api` for your Prismic repository.

Your app can look things up in this if you need to (for example, to resolve
links using the bookmarks `Dict`).
aliases.type: { refs : List RefProperties, bookmarks : Dict String String, types : Dict String String, tags : List String, version : String, forms : Dict String Form, oauthInitiate : String, oauthToken : String, license : String, experiments : Experiments }

aliases.name: Decoder
aliases.comment:
Decoders are parameterized by the input type `val` (`Document`, `Field`,
`Group` or `Slice`) and the result type `a` -- your type representing your
custom Prismic document type.

aliases.name: Document
aliases.comment:
Holds the Prismic document.

`Documents` consist of basic `Fields`, `Groups` and `Slices`.

You will decode this into your own document type by passing a `Decoder Document
MyDocType` to `submit`.

aliases.name: Experiments
aliases.comment: TODO: Experiments are not Strings. Fill out this type.
aliases.type: { draft : List String, running : List String }

aliases.name: Form
aliases.comment:
Properties representing a Prismic form.

These are used to construct a default query.
aliases.type: { method : String, enctype : String, action : String, fields : Dict String FormField, rel : Maybe String, name : Maybe String }

aliases.name: FormField
aliases.comment:
A field in a Prismic form.

These are combined to construct query parameters for the eventual Http request.
aliases.type: { fieldType : FieldType, multiple : Bool, default : Maybe String }

aliases.name: RefProperties
aliases.comment:
Properties representing a Prismic ref.

Most of the time you will be working with the `master` ref, which is added to
all requests by default.
aliases.type: { id : String, ref : Ref, label : String, isMasterRef : Bool }

aliases.name: Response
aliases.comment:
Represents a Prismic response.

This type is parameterized by `docType`, which is determined by the `Decoder`
you pass to `submit`.
aliases.type: { license : String, nextPage : Maybe String, page : Int, prevPage : Maybe String, results : List docType, resultsPerPage : Int, resultsSize : Int, totalPages : Int, totalResultsSize : Int, version : String }

values.name: andThen
values.type: (a -> Decoder val b) -> Decoder val a -> Decoder val b

values.name: any
values.comment: `any fragment values` matches documents having any of `values` at `fragment`.

values.name: api
values.comment:
Go and fetch the Prismic `Api` metadata, if it has not already been fetched.

The `Api` is cached in the returned `Model`, so we don't have to fetch it next
time.

You start every Prismic request with this function.

values.name: apply
values.type: Decoder val (a -> b) -> Decoder val a -> Decoder val b

values.name: at
values.comment: `at fragment value` matches documents having `value` at `fragment`.

values.name: atL
values.comment: `atL fragment values` matches documents having a list of `values` at `fragment`.

values.name: bookmark
values.comment: Convenience function for fetching a bookmarked document.
values.type: String -> Task PrismicError ( Model, Api ) -> Task PrismicError Request

values.name: cache
values.comment:
The `submit` `Task` returns an updated Prismic `Model` with the request and
response cached.

In your app's `update` function, you should merge this with the existing cache
using `cache`.

    update msg model =
        case msg of
            MyPrismicMsg (Ok ( prismic, response )) ->
                { model
                    | prismic =
                        cache model.prismic prismic
                }

values.name: custom
values.comment:
Use a standard decoder in a pipeline.

The following is equivalent to the example using `required`:

    myDocDecoder : Decoder Document MyDoc
    myDocDecoder =
        decode MyDoc
            |> custom (requiredField "title" structuredText)
values.type: Decoder val a -> Decoder val (a -> b) -> Decoder val b

values.name: decode
values.comment:
Begin a decoding pipeline.

    type alias MyDoc =
        { title : StructuredText }

    myDocDecoder : Decoder Document MyDoc
    myDocDecoder =
        decode MyDoc
            |> required "title" structuredText

values.name: form
values.comment: Choose a form on which to base the rest of the Prismic request.
values.type: String -> Task PrismicError ( Model, Api ) -> Task PrismicError Request

values.name: fulltext
values.comment: `fulltext fragment value` matches documents with a full text search at `fragment`.

values.name: group
values.comment: Pipeline version of `groupField`.
values.type: String -> Decoder Group a -> Decoder Document (List a -> b) -> Decoder Document b

values.name: groupField
values.comment:
Decode a group.

Pass this function a `Decoder Group a` from the `Prismic.Group` module.

Groups can contain Fields, but not other Groups or Slices.

Here is an example with a document containing a group:

    type alias MyDoc =
        { albums : List Album }

    type alias Album =
        { title : String
        , cover : Field.ImageViews
        }

    albumDecoder : Decoder Group Album
    albumDecoder =
        Prismic.decode Album
            |> Group.required "title" Field.text
            |> Group.required "cover" Field.image

    myDocDecoder : Decoder Document MyDoc
    myDocDecoder =
        Prismic.map MyDoc
            (Prismic.groupField "albums" albumDecoder)
values.type: String -> Decoder Group a -> Decoder Document (List a)

values.name: href
values.comment: The document's href.

values.name: id
values.comment: The document's ID.

values.name: init
values.comment:
Initialise the Prismic model with the URL for your Prismic repository. Save
this in your application's Model somewhere.

    type alias Model =
        { prismic : Prismic.Model }

    init =
        { prismic =
            Prismic.init "https://lesbonneschoses.prismic.io/api"
        }

values.name: initWith
values.comment: Initialise with custom options.

values.name: lang
values.comment: Override a Form's default lang.
values.type: String -> Task PrismicError Request -> Task PrismicError Request

values.name: linkedDocuments
values.comment: The document's linked documents.

values.name: map
values.comment: Transform a decoder.
values.type: (a -> b) -> Decoder val a -> Decoder val b

values.name: optional
values.comment: Decode a field that might be missing.
values.type: String -> Decoder Field a -> a -> Decoder Document (a -> b) -> Decoder Document b

values.name: optionalField
values.comment: Decode a field that might be missing.
values.type: String -> Decoder Field a -> a -> Decoder Document a

values.name: query
values.comment:
Override a Form's default query.

See the section on `Predicate`s below for how to construct a `Predicate`.
values.type: List Predicate -> Task PrismicError Request -> Task PrismicError Request

values.name: ref
values.comment: Override a Form's default ref
values.type: String -> Task PrismicError Request -> Task PrismicError Request

values.name: required
values.comment: Decode a required field.
values.type: String -> Decoder Field a -> Decoder Document (a -> b) -> Decoder Document b

values.name: requiredField
values.comment:
Decode a field.

Pass this function a `Decoder Field a` from the `Prismic.Field` module.
values.type: String -> Decoder Field a -> Decoder Document a

values.name: sliceZone
values.comment: Pipeline version of `sliceZoneField`.
values.type: String -> Decoder Slice a -> Decoder Document (List a -> b) -> Decoder Document b

values.name: sliceZoneField
values.comment:
Decode a SliceZone.

Pass this function a `Decoder Slice a` from the `Prismic.Slice` module.

Slices can contain Fields and Groups, but not other Slices.

    type alias MyDoc =
        { sections : List Section }

    type Section
        = -- The "my-content" slice has a non-repeating zone.
          MyContent Field.StructuredText
        | -- The "my-image-gallery" slice has a repeating zone.
          MyImageGallery (List Field.ImageViews)
        | -- The "my-links-section" slice has both non-repeating and repeating
          -- zones.
          MyLinksSection LinksSection

    type alias LinksSection =
        { title : Field.StructuredText
        , links : List Field.Link
        }

    myDocDecoder : Decoder Document MyDoc
    myDocDecoder =
        Prismic.map MyDoc
            (Prismic.sliceZoneField "sections" sectionDecoder)

    sectionDecoder : Decoder Slice Section
    sectionDecoder =
        Slice.oneOf
            [ Slice.slice "my-content"
                -- Decode the non-repeating zone and ignore the repeating zone.
                (Group.field "text" Field.structuredText)
                (Prismic.succeed ())
                |> Prismic.map (\( content, _ ) -> MyContent content)
            , Slice.slice "my-image-gallery"
                -- Ignore the non-repeating zone and decode the repeating zone.
                (Prismic.succeed ())
                (Group.field "image" Field.image)
                |> Prismic.map (\( _, images ) -> MyImageGallery images)
            , Slice.slice "my-links-section"
                -- Decode both the non-repeating and repeating zones.
                (Group.field "title" Field.structuredText)
                (Group.field "link" Field.link)
                |> Prismic.map
                    (\( title, links ) -> MyLinksSection (LinksSection title links))
            ]
values.type: String -> Decoder Slice a -> Decoder Document (List a)

values.name: slugs
values.comment: The document's slugs.

values.name: submit
values.comment:
Submit the request.

Pass this function a `Decoder` to decode each document in the response into your
own Elm document type.
values.type: Decoder Document docType -> Task PrismicError Request -> Task PrismicError ( Model, Response docType )

values.name: tags
values.comment: The document's tags.

values.name: uid
values.comment: The document's UID.

name: Prismic.Slice

values.name: labelledV1Slice
values.comment:
Decode a (deprecated) old-style slice in a slice zone. The tagger is also passed the slice label.

TODO: custom label decoders?
values.type: String -> (Maybe String -> a -> b) -> FieldDecoder a -> Decoder Slice b

values.name: oneOf
values.type: List (Decoder Slice a) -> Decoder Slice a

values.name: slice
values.comment:
Decode a slice in a slice zone.

Slices contain a non-repeating zone and a repeating zone. To decode a Slice, you
pass a `Decoder Group a` (for the non-repeating zone) and a `Decoder Group b`
(for the repeating zone), and you'll get back a `Decoder Slice (a, List b)`.

If your slice doesn't have one of the zones, you can just ignore it by passing a
decoder that always succeeds: `succeed ()`.
values.type: String -> Decoder Group a -> Decoder Group b -> Decoder Slice ( a, List b )

values.name: v1Slice
values.comment: Decode a (deprecated) old-style slice in a slice zone.
values.type: String -> (a -> b) -> FieldDecoder a -> Decoder Slice b

name: Prismic.Group

aliases.name: Group
aliases.comment:
A `Group` is a collection of basic `Fields`.

`Groups` cannot contain `Slices` or other `Groups`.

values.name: field
values.comment: Decode a field
values.type: String -> Decoder Field a -> Decoder Group a

values.name: optional
values.type: String -> Decoder Field a -> a -> Decoder Group (a -> b) -> Decoder Group b

values.name: optionalField
values.comment: Decode a field that might be missing.
values.type: String -> Decoder Field a -> a -> Decoder Group a

values.name: required
values.type: String -> Decoder Field a -> Decoder Group (a -> b) -> Decoder Group b

name: Prismic.Field

aliases.name: DocumentReference
aliases.comment: A reference to a Prismic document.

aliases.name: ImageView
aliases.comment: Properties for a single image view.

aliases.name: Link
aliases.comment: Links to other documents or to the web.

aliases.name: LinkResolver
aliases.comment:
A `LinkResolver` simply converts a Prismic `DocumentReference` to a list of
`Html.Attribute`s. `structuredTextAsHtml` and friends add these attributes to
links in the text.

For example, you can use this to add `onClick` handlers to links:

    type Msg
        = NavigateTo DocumentReference

    myLinkResolver : LinkResolver Msg
    myLinkResolver docRef =
        [ Html.Attributes.href ""
        , Html.Events.onClick (NavigateTo docRef)
        ]

    view : StructuredText -> Html Msg
    view myStructuredText =
        structuredTextAsHtml myLinkResolver myStructuredText

Your `update` function would handle the `NavigateTo` message and perform the
appropriate routing.
aliases.type: { resolveDocumentReference : DocumentReference -> List (Attribute msg), resolveUrl : String -> List (Attribute msg) }

aliases.name: StructuredText
aliases.comment: `StructuredText` can be rendered to HTML using `structuredTextAsHtml`.

values.name: date
values.comment: Decode a Date field.

values.name: defaultLinkResolver
values.comment:
Adds a default `href` attribute to links:
[ href "documents/{doc.id}/{doc.slug}" ]

values.name: getFirstImage
values.comment: Get the first image out of some `StructuredText`, if there is one.

values.name: getFirstParagraph
values.comment: Get the first paragraph out of some `StructuredText`, if there is one.

values.name: getText
values.comment: Get the contents of a single `StructuredText` element as a `String`.

values.name: getTexts
values.comment: Get the contents of a some `StructuredText` as a `String`.

values.name: getTitle
values.comment: Get the first title out of some `StructuredText`, if there is one.

values.name: image
values.comment: Decode an Image field.

values.name: link
values.comment: Decode a Link field.

values.name: structuredText
values.comment: Decode a StructuredText field.

values.name: structuredTextAsHtml
values.comment:
Render some `StructuredText` as HTML.

You must supply a `LinkResolver` to resolve any links in the `StructuredText`.
If you don't care about this, you can use the `defaultLinkResolver`.

values.name: structuredTextBlockAsHtml
values.comment: Render a single block of `StructuredText` as HTML.

values.name: text
values.comment: Decode a Text field.
